title: Epic: Migrate OAuth scopes from transitional to granular (production only)
description: Currently OAUTH_SCOPE is a single constant 'atproto transition:generic' used in all environments. For production, we should use granular scopes that precisely describe what the app needs: repo access to hypercert collections + profiles, blob uploads, and RPC calls. For loopback (local dev), transition:generic must remain (ATProto requirement for loopback clients). The granular scope constants already exist in lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts (HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE, RPC_SCOPE, BLOB_SCOPE) but are unused. However, HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE is incomplete — it's missing collections the app writes to (app.certified.actor.profile, app.bsky.actor.profile, org.hypercerts.claim.attachment, org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails, org.hypercerts.claim.contributorInformation, org.hypercerts.helper.workScopeTag). Success: production deployments request only the granular permissions needed; loopback still uses transition:generic; all existing functionality works.

title: Update HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE to include all collections the app writes to
description:
## Files
- lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts (modify)

## What to do
The `HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE` constant (line 11-12) is missing several collections that the app and SDK internally write to. Update it to include ALL collections from `HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS` that the app needs write access to.

Current scope has these collections:
- org.hypercerts.claim.activity ✓
- org.hypercerts.claim.contribution ✓  
- org.hypercerts.claim.evaluation ✓
- org.hypercerts.claim.evidence ✓
- org.hypercerts.claim.measurement ✓
- org.hypercerts.claim.collection ✓
- org.hypercerts.claim.rights ✓
- org.hypercerts.claim.funding ✓
- app.certified.location ✓

Add these missing collections:
- org.hypercerts.claim.attachment (used by addAttachment in app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts)
- org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails (SDK creates these internally during hypercert creation with contributions)
- org.hypercerts.claim.contributorInformation (SDK creates these internally during hypercert creation with contributions)
- org.hypercerts.helper.workScopeTag (SDK may create these for work scope references)
- app.certified.actor.profile (used by profile create/update in app/api/profile/update/route.ts)
- app.bsky.actor.profile (used by bsky profile create/update in app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts)

Use `HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS` from `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core` to build the scope string programmatically instead of hardcoding collection names. Import it and construct the scope like:

```typescript
import { HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core";

const REPO_COLLECTIONS = [
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CLAIM,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.RIGHTS,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.LOCATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CONTRIBUTION_DETAILS,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CONTRIBUTOR_INFORMATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.MEASUREMENT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.EVALUATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.ATTACHMENT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.COLLECTION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.FUNDING_RECEIPT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.WORK_SCOPE_TAG,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.BSKY_PROFILE,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CERTIFIED_PROFILE,
];

const collectionParams = REPO_COLLECTIONS.map(c => `collection=${c}`).join("&");
export const HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE = `repo?${collectionParams}&action=create&action=update&action=delete`;
```

Also remove the comment "// Future granular scopes (not yet used)" on line 10 since they will now be used.

## Don't
- Change RPC_SCOPE or BLOB_SCOPE — they are already correct
- Change how the SDK is initialized
- Change any exports or the default export
notes: Merged into hypercerts-02l.2 — scope definition and env-switching are one atomic change

title: Make OAUTH_SCOPE environment-aware: granular for production, transitional for loopback
description:
## Files
- lib/config.ts (modify)
- lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts (modify)

## What to do
Currently `OAUTH_SCOPE` in `lib/config.ts` (line 19) is a single constant:
```typescript
export const OAUTH_SCOPE = [ATPROTO_SCOPE, TRANSITION_SCOPES.GENERIC].join(" ");
```

This is used everywhere regardless of environment. Change it so:
- **Loopback (local dev):** keeps `"atproto transition:generic"` (ATProto requirement)
- **Production:** uses the granular scopes: `ATPROTO_SCOPE + HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE + RPC_SCOPE + BLOB_SCOPE`

### In lib/config.ts:

1. Import `HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE`, `RPC_SCOPE`, `BLOB_SCOPE` from `./hypercerts-sdk` — BUT this creates a circular dependency since `hypercerts-sdk.ts` imports from `config.ts`. Instead, move the scope constants into `config.ts` or create a new file. The cleanest approach: move the granular scope construction into `config.ts` itself.

   Import `HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS` from `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core` (already importing from there) and build the scopes in `config.ts`:

```typescript
import { ATPROTO_SCOPE, TRANSITION_SCOPES, HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core";

// Granular repo scope — all collections the app needs write access to
const REPO_COLLECTIONS = [
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CLAIM,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.RIGHTS,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.LOCATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CONTRIBUTION_DETAILS,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CONTRIBUTOR_INFORMATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.MEASUREMENT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.EVALUATION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.ATTACHMENT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.COLLECTION,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.FUNDING_RECEIPT,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.WORK_SCOPE_TAG,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.BSKY_PROFILE,
  HYPERCERT_COLLECTIONS.CERTIFIED_PROFILE,
];

const HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE = `repo?${REPO_COLLECTIONS.map(c => "collection=" + c).join("&")}&action=create&action=update&action=delete`;
const RPC_SCOPE = "rpc:app.bsky.actor.getProfile?aud=did:web:api.bsky.app%23bsky_appview";
const BLOB_SCOPE = "blob?accept=video/*&accept=image/*";

const GRANULAR_SCOPE = [ATPROTO_SCOPE, HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE, RPC_SCOPE, BLOB_SCOPE].join(" ");
const LOOPBACK_SCOPE = [ATPROTO_SCOPE, TRANSITION_SCOPES.GENERIC].join(" ");
```

2. Change `OAUTH_SCOPE` to be a function of the base URL (loopback detection already exists in this file):

```typescript
function getOAuthScope(baseUrl: string): string {
  if (isLoopback(baseUrl)) {
    return LOOPBACK_SCOPE;
  }
  return GRANULAR_SCOPE;
}
```

3. Replace the current `OAUTH_SCOPE` constant (line 19) with the function call. Since `baseUrl` is computed at module level (line 154), place the scope computation after it:

```typescript
export const OAUTH_SCOPE = getOAuthScope(baseUrl);
```

   Move this line to after `const baseUrl = getBaseUrl();` (around line 154), or keep the export at the top and make it lazy. The simplest: move the export to after line 154.

4. Update the JSDoc comment above OAUTH_SCOPE to explain the dual behavior.

### In lib/hypercerts-sdk.ts:

5. Remove the now-redundant scope constants (`HYPERCERT_REPO_SCOPE`, `RPC_SCOPE`, `BLOB_SCOPE`) since they are now defined in `config.ts`. Keep the `export { OAUTH_SCOPE }` re-export.

## Don't
- Change the loopback detection logic
- Change how `buildClientId` works (it already receives scope as a parameter)
- Change the SDK initialization (it already uses `config.scope`)
- Change `client-metadata.json/route.ts` — it already imports OAUTH_SCOPE from config
- Break the circular dependency: config.ts must NOT import from hypercerts-sdk.ts
close_reason: a3b2ede Make OAUTH_SCOPE environment-aware: granular for production, loopback for dev

title: Fix BLOB_SCOPE to allow all MIME types, not just image/* and video/*
description:
## Files
- lib/config.ts (modify)

## What to do
Line 38 currently restricts blob uploads to only images and videos:
```typescript
const BLOB_SCOPE = "blob?accept=video/*&accept=image/*";
```

The app uploads attachments of arbitrary types (markdown, PDF, GeoJSON, etc.) via the add-attachment flow. This causes the error: `Failed to upload blob: Missing required scope "blob:text/markdown"`.

Change BLOB_SCOPE to use the wildcard MIME type `*/*` which matches any content type:
```typescript
const BLOB_SCOPE = "blob:*/*";
```

Note the syntax difference: `blob:*/*` (colon, single MIME pattern) vs `blob?accept=...` (query params, multiple patterns). The `blob:*/*` form is the correct ATProto scope syntax for a single wildcard MIME type. The SDK's `hasPermission` function confirms `blob:*/*` matches any specific MIME type like `blob:text/markdown`.

## Don't
- Change any other scope constants
- Add individual MIME types — use the wildcard instead
close_reason: b403f8b fix BLOB_SCOPE to use wildcard */* for all MIME types

title: Epic: Improve error messages across the scaffold
description: Users (including the developer) see generic errors like 'Failed to add measurement', 'Authentication failed. Please try again.', and 'Unable to get repository context' with zero context about what actually went wrong. This makes debugging impossible without checking server logs. Success looks like: every user-facing error toast includes a specific reason (e.g. 'Failed to add measurement: session expired, please log in again'), and every server-side throw includes the operation name and relevant IDs. Key constraint: don't expose internal implementation details or secrets in user-facing messages.

title: Remove raw error leak in logout route
description:
## Files
- app/api/auth/logout/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: security issue at line 21.

**Original comment:**
> Raw error message leaked to the client. `(e as Error).message` may expose internal details (e.g., Redis connection errors, session store internals) in the API response. This is also inconsistent with the callback route which deliberately uses a generic error code. Additionally, if `e` is not an `Error` instance, `.message` will be `undefined`.

**Suggested fix:**
1. Replace the error response to use a generic message instead of the raw error:
```diff
     return NextResponse.json(
-      { error: `Logout failed: ${(e as Error).message}` },
+      { error: "Logout failed" },
       { status: 500 },
     );
```
2. Keep the existing server-side logging of the full error (the console.error on the line above).

**Context:**
The logout route catch block on line 17 already logs the full error server-side with console.error. The client response should not include the raw error message. The auth callback route already uses a generic error pattern — this should be consistent.

CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2818882953

## Dont
- Dont change the console.error logging line
- Dont change the HTTP status code (keep 500)
- Dont change the try block logic
- Dont introduce new dependencies
close_reason: b2c46cb fix: remove raw error leak in logout route

title: Fix loading/error state race in evaluations section
description:
## Files - components/hypercert-evaluations-section.tsx (modify) ## What to do CodeRabbit flagged: bug at line 87. **Original comment:** > Loading and error states can render simultaneously. If `isLoadingLinks` is false but `isLoadingDetails` is true while `isErrorLinks` is also true, both the skeleton and error panel will render at the same time. Consider using an if/else-if chain or guarding error rendering with `!isLoading`. **Suggested fix:** Replace the three separate conditional blocks (lines 75-93) with a single if/else-if chain: ```tsx {isLoading ? (
) : isError ? (

Failed to load evaluations.

) : ( <> {evaluations && evaluations.length > 0 ? ( // ... existing content rendering ) : ( // ... existing empty state )} )} ``` The key change: the three separate `{isLoading ? ... : null}`, `{isError ? ... : null}`, `{!isLoading && !isError ? ... : null}` blocks become a single ternary chain so only one state renders at a time. **Context:** The component has `isLoading` (combined from isLoadingLinks and isLoadingDetails) and `isError` (combined from isErrorLinks and isErrorDetails). Currently lines 75-93 render them as three independent blocks, allowing skeleton and error to show simultaneously. title: Fix loose includes(https) URL check in measurement view
description:
## Files
- components/hypercert-measurement-view.tsx (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug at line 118.

**Original comment:**
> `includes("https")` is a loose URL protocol check. `uri?.includes("https")` would match any URI containing "https" anywhere in the string (e.g., a path segment). Use `uri?.startsWith("https")` for a more precise protocol check.

**Suggested fix:**
Replace on line 117:
```diff
-                    uri={uri?.includes("https") ? uri : getPDSlsURI(uri)}
+                    uri={uri?.startsWith("https") ? uri : getPDSlsURI(uri)}
```

**Context:**
The URILink component receives a uri prop. The check determines whether to use the uri directly (if it is an https URL) or to wrap it with getPDSlsURI(). Using includes would incorrectly match URIs like "at://did:plc:xxx/app.certified.https.something" that contain "https" in a path segment.

CodeRabbit comment: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-scaffold-atproto/pull/7#discussion_r2818882990

## Dont
- Dont change the URILink component itself
- Dont change getPDSlsURI function
- Dont change other files (note: hypercert-evaluation-view.tsx has similar patterns but is NOT in scope of this comment)
- Dont introduce new dependencies
close_reason: 87309cc Fix loose includes(https) URL check in measurement view

Fix: error detail still leaked to client in add-attachment and bsky/update routes (from hypercerts-dcb.5)
description:
Review of hypercerts-dcb.5 found: Two API routes still interpolate the raw error message directly into the client-facing JSON response, leaking internal error details.

**app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts line 142:**
```ts
error: `Failed to add attachment: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}`
```

**app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts line 107:**
```ts
error: `Profile update failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`
```

Both were fixed by hypercerts-tlw.5 (commit 81cca2f) to use the safe instanceof check, but the fix only replaced the unsafe cast — it still interpolates the message into the client response. The dcb.5 spec requires that error details stay server-side only (in console.error) and the client receives only a generic message.

**Required fix:**
- add-attachment: return `{ error: "Failed to add attachment" }` (no interpolation)
- bsky/update: return `{ error: "Profile update failed" }` (no interpolation)
- Keep existing console.error calls (they already log the full error object)

**Evidence:** git show 81cca2f -- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts
close_reason: 2acaf31 fix: remove error detail leak from client responses

title: Fix profile picture going blank after update by applying CDN URL conversion
description:
## Files
- app/api/profile/update/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts (modify)
- providers/SignedInProvider.tsx (modify)

## What to do
After a profile picture update, the image goes blank because the API response returns raw blob URLs (e.g. XRPC getBlob URLs from PDS hosts) that are not in Next.js allowed remotePatterns. The server-side profile page (app/profile/page.tsx) correctly applies `convertBlobUrlToCdn()` from `lib/utils.ts` before passing URLs as props, but the API routes do not.

### Fix 1: app/api/profile/update/route.ts
Around lines 99-115, the route fetches the updated profile and returns avatar/banner URLs. Currently:
```ts
const avatarUrl = updated?.avatar || "";
const bannerUrl = updated?.banner || "";
```
Change to:
```ts
import { convertBlobUrlToCdn } from "@/lib/utils";
// ...
const avatarUrl = convertBlobUrlToCdn(updated?.avatar) || "";
const bannerUrl = convertBlobUrlToCdn(updated?.banner) || "";
```

### Fix 2: app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts
Same pattern — find where avatar and banner URLs are returned (around lines 86-87) and apply `convertBlobUrlToCdn()` to both.

### Fix 3: providers/SignedInProvider.tsx
Around line 30, the Navbar avatar URL is set from the raw profile:
```ts
avatarUrl = profile?.avatar;
```
Change to:
```ts
import { convertBlobUrlToCdn } from "@/lib/utils";
// ...
avatarUrl = convertBlobUrlToCdn(profile?.avatar) || "";
```

## Dont
- Do NOT change the convertBlobUrlToCdn function itself
- Do NOT modify the profile-form.tsx onSuccess handler (the fix should come from the API returning correct URLs)
- Do NOT modify app/profile/page.tsx (it already works correctly)
- Do NOT add new entries to next.config.ts remotePatterns — the CDN URL conversion is the correct approach
- Do NOT change the ImageUploader component

title: Fix avatar camera icon getting clipped on profile page
description:
## Files - components/image-uploader.tsx (modify) ## What to do When the avatar has an image, the camera icon overlay button (the edit/replace button) is positioned at `bottom-0 right-0` inside a container that has `overflow-hidden` and `rounded-full`. Because the circle clips the corners, the camera button gets cut off — it sits in the corner of the bounding box but the circle masks it. The fix: move the camera icon button **outside** the overflow-hidden container so it is not clipped, but still visually anchored to the bottom-right of the avatar circle. ### Approach In `image-uploader.tsx`, for the `aspect="square"` (avatar) case only: 1. The camera button (lines 65-74) needs to sit outside the `overflow-hidden` container but still be positioned relative to it. The simplest approach: wrap the entire component in a `relative` container and move the camera button to be a sibling of the overflow-hidden div rather than a child of it. 2. Specifically for the avatar case (`!isBanner`), the camera button should be positioned at the bottom-right edge of the circle. A good position is `bottom-0 right-0` on the outer relative wrapper (which does NOT have overflow-hidden). 3. For the banner case (`isBanner`), keep the current behavior (`bottom-2 right-2` inside the overflow container) since banners are rectangular and dont clip. 4. Make sure the camera button circle is fully visible — the entire round button with the camera icon should be shown, not cut off. ### Current structure (simplified): ```
``` ### Target structure for avatar: ```
``` For banner, keep the button inside the overflow container as it is today. ## Dont - Do NOT change the avatar size (w-24 h-24) - Do NOT change the banner layout or positioning - Do NOT change the profile-form.tsx file - Do NOT remove the ring-4 ring-background styling (that comes from profile-form.tsx parent) - Do NOT change the camera icon size or styling (bg-black/40, backdrop-blur, etc.) — only change its positioning/nesting

title: Fix: JWK private key stripping — switch denylist to allowlist
description:
## Files
- app/jwks.json/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: security issue at line 68.

**Original comment:**
> Private key stripping may be incomplete for RSA JWKs. The filter only removes d, use, and key_ops. For EC keys this is sufficient. However, if RSA keys are ever used, the fields p, q, dp, dq, qi, and oth are also private and would be leaked through this endpoint. Consider using an allowlist of public fields instead of a denylist.

**Suggested fix:**
Replace the PRIVATE_KEY_FIELDS denylist with a PUBLIC_JWK_FIELDS allowlist. Define:
```ts
const PUBLIC_JWK_FIELDS = ["kty", "crv", "x", "y", "n", "e", "kid", "alg"] as const;
```
Then filter entries to keep only keys in PUBLIC_JWK_FIELDS, and append `key_ops: ["verify"]` to each result.

**Context:**
This is the JWKS endpoint that exposes public keys for OAuth signature verification. Leaking private key material would be a critical security issue. The current denylist approach is fragile — an allowlist is safer.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in the same file
- Don't modify the key generation or storage logic
- Don't change the route handler signature or response structure
close_reason: 9555b4f fix: replace PRIVATE_KEY_FIELDS denylist with PUBLIC_JWK_FIELDS allowlist in jwks.json route — already implemented as part of hypercerts-1hr.3

title: Fix: loose includes("https") URL check in measurement view
description:
## Files
- components/hypercert-measurement-view.tsx (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: bug at line 118.

**Original comment:**
> uri?.includes("https") is a loose URL protocol check. It would match any URI containing "https" anywhere in the string (e.g., a path segment). Use uri?.startsWith("https") for a more precise protocol check.

**Suggested fix:**
Change:
```tsx
uri={uri?.includes("https") ? uri : getPDSlsURI(uri)}
```
To:
```tsx
uri={uri?.startsWith("https") ? uri : getPDSlsURI(uri)}
```

**Context:**
The URILink component receives a uri prop. If the URI is already an https URL, it's used directly; otherwise it's converted via getPDSlsURI(). The current includes() check could incorrectly match URIs like `at://user.example/https-path`.

## Don't

- Don't modify the URILink or getPDSlsURI implementations
- Don't change other URI checks elsewhere in the file

title: Fix: safe error handling across 3 API routes (add-location, profile/update, auth/logout)
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/profile/update/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/auth/logout/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged 3 related error-handling issues:

1. [error-handling] Line 102 in add-location/route.ts: `(e as Error).message` leaks internal error details to the client and is undefined if e is not an Error instance. Replace with safe extraction: `const detail = e instanceof Error ? e.message : "Unknown error";` and use a generic message in the response (do NOT include the raw error message in the client response — keep it server-side only via console.error which is already present).

2. [error-handling] Line 128 in profile/update/route.ts: Same `(error as Error).message` unsafe cast pattern. Replace with `error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)` for the server-side log, and return only a generic error message to the client.

3. [error-handling] Line 21 in auth/logout/route.ts: Raw error message leaked via template literal `Logout failed: ${(e as Error).message}`. Change to return only `{ error: "Logout failed" }` without the raw message. Keep the existing server-side logging.

**Suggested fixes:**
For all 3 routes:
- Use `e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)` for server-side logging
- Return only generic error messages to the client (no `.message` interpolation)
- Keep existing console.error/logging calls intact

**Context:**
These are API routes that catch errors and return JSON responses. Leaking internal error messages (Redis connection errors, SDK internals, file paths) to API consumers is a security concern. The server-side logging already captures the full error.

## Don't
- Don't change unrelated code in these files
- Don't remove the existing console.error/logging calls
- Don't change the HTTP status codes
- Don't introduce new dependencies

title: Epic: Add ePDS email/OTP login (scrappy e2e)
description:
Add ePDS email/OTP login as a second login option alongside the existing handle-based SDK login. The goal is to validate the full flow end-to-end as fast as possible — especially that manually constructing a NodeSavedSession and writing it to Redis works with sdk.restoreSession(). Polish comes later.

Success looks like: a user can click 'Sign in with email', enter their email, get redirected to ePDS, enter the OTP, get redirected back, and the app recognizes them as logged in (getSession/getAuthenticatedRepo work).

Key constraints:
- ePDS uses a separate PDS URL (NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL env var)
- sdk.authorize() cannot be used because it requires a handle, not an email
- We must do PAR + PKCE + DPoP manually for the login handler
- sdk.callback() cannot be used because it expects state in Redis (written by sdk.authorize)
- After manual token exchange, we construct a NodeSavedSession and write it to RedisSessionStore so sdk.restoreSession() works transparently
- The user-did cookie is set identically to the existing flow
- UX is scrappy — just needs to work, not look good

title: Create lib/epds-helpers.ts — PKCE + DPoP utilities
description:
## Files
- lib/epds-helpers.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a new file with the following exports, using only `node:crypto` (no new dependencies):

1. `generateCodeVerifier()` — returns `crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('base64url')`
2. `generateCodeChallenge(verifier: string)` — returns SHA-256 hash of verifier as base64url
3. `generateState()` — returns `crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('base64url')`
4. `generateDpopKeyPair()` — generates EC P-256 key pair, returns `{ privateKey: KeyObject, publicJwk: JsonWebKey, privateJwk: JsonWebKey }`
5. `restoreDpopKeyPair(privateJwk: JsonWebKey)` — recreates KeyObject from serialized JWK, returns `{ privateKey: KeyObject, publicJwk: JsonWebKey }`
6. `createDpopProof(opts: { privateKey: KeyObject, jwk: object, method: string, url: string, nonce?: string, accessToken?: string })` — creates a DPoP proof JWT (ES256, typ: dpop+jwt). If nonce provided, include in payload. If accessToken provided, include `ath` (SHA-256 hash of token, base64url).
7. Internal `derToRaw(der: Buffer)` — converts DER-encoded ECDSA signature to raw r||s format (64 bytes). Not exported.

The exact implementation is in the ePDS tutorial: https://github.com/hypercerts-org/ePDS/blob/main/docs/tutorial.md — copy it verbatim with proper TypeScript types.

Also export a convenience function:
8. `fetchWithDpopRetry(url: string, body: URLSearchParams, privateKey: KeyObject, publicJwk: object): Promise<Response>` — wraps the nonce retry pattern (first request without nonce, if 400 + dpop-nonce header, retry with nonce).

## Don't
- Add any npm dependencies
- Use jose or any other JWT library — use raw node:crypto only
- Export derToRaw

## Files

## What to do
Create a module that derives ePDS OAuth endpoints from the config.

Export a function `getEpdsEndpoints()` that reads `config.epdsUrl` and returns:
```typescript

  authEndpoint: string   // https://auth.${hostname}/oauth/authorize
```

Example: if `NEXT_PUBLIC_EPDS_URL=https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app`:
- PAR: `https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app/oauth/par`
- Auth: `https://auth.pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app/oauth/authorize`
- Token: `https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app/oauth/token`

Also export `getEpdsClientId()` which returns `config.clientId` (same client ID as the existing flow — the app is the same OAuth client regardless of login method).

And `getEpdsRedirectUri()` which returns `config.epdsRedirectUri`.

Throw a clear error if `config.epdsUrl` is not set when these functions are called.

title: Create lib/epds-session-cookie.ts — signed transient OAuth cookie
description:
## Files
- lib/epds-session-cookie.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a module for encoding/decoding a signed HttpOnly cookie that stores transient OAuth state during the ePDS login flow. This cookie lives for 10 minutes (matching PAR request_uri lifetime) and is deleted after the callback.

### Types
```typescript
interface EpdsOAuthSessionData {
  state: string
  codeVerifier: string
  dpopPrivateJwk: JsonWebKey  // from node:crypto
}
```

### Functions

1. `encodeEpdsSessionCookie(data: EpdsOAuthSessionData): string`
   - JSON.stringify the data
   - Compute HMAC-SHA256 of the JSON using `config.oauthSessionSecret`
   - Return `${base64url(json)}.${base64url(hmac)}`
   - Throw if `config.oauthSessionSecret` is not set

2. `decodeEpdsSessionCookie(cookie: string): EpdsOAuthSessionData`
   - Split on '.'
   - Verify HMAC matches
   - Parse and return the data
   - Throw 'Invalid session cookie' if HMAC doesn't match or parsing fails

### Cookie name
Export `EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME = 'epds-oauth-session'`

## Don't
- Add any npm dependencies (use node:crypto only)
- Store the cookie yourself — just encode/decode the value. The route handlers will set/delete the cookie.
- Use JWT format — the simple payload.signature format is sufficient
close_reason: f089177 Add lib/epds-session-cookie.ts — signed transient OAuth cookie

title: Create app/api/oauth/login/route.ts — ePDS PAR + redirect
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/login/route.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a GET route handler: `GET /api/oauth/login?email=user@example.com`

### Flow
1. Read `email` from `req.nextUrl.searchParams.get('email')`. If missing, return 400 JSON error.
2. Call `generateDpopKeyPair()`, `generateCodeVerifier()`, `generateCodeChallenge(verifier)`, `generateState()` from `@/lib/epds-helpers`.
3. Get endpoints from `getEpdsEndpoints()`, client ID from `getEpdsClientId()`, redirect URI from `getEpdsRedirectUri()` — all from `@/lib/epds-config`.
4. Build PAR body as URLSearchParams:
   - client_id, redirect_uri, response_type: 'code', scope: 'atproto transition:generic', state, code_challenge, code_challenge_method: 'S256', login_hint: email
5. Call `fetchWithDpopRetry(parEndpoint, parBody, privateKey, publicJwk)` from `@/lib/epds-helpers`.
6. If PAR response is not ok, return 502 JSON error with status text.
7. Parse `{ request_uri }` from PAR response JSON.
8. Encode session cookie: `encodeEpdsSessionCookie({ state, codeVerifier, dpopPrivateJwk: privateJwk })`
9. Build auth URL: `${authEndpoint}?client_id=${clientId}&request_uri=${request_uri}&login_hint=${email}` (all URI-encoded)
10. Create `NextResponse.redirect(authUrl)`
11. Set cookie on the response: name=EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME, value=encoded cookie, httpOnly=true, secure=config.isProduction, sameSite='lax', maxAge=600, path='/'
12. Return the redirect response.

### Imports
- `NextRequest`, `NextResponse` from 'next/server'
- helpers from `@/lib/epds-helpers`
- config functions from `@/lib/epds-config`
- cookie functions from `@/lib/epds-session-cookie`
- `config` from `@/lib/config` (for isProduction)

## Don't
- Accept POST — this is GET only (browser redirect)
- Use sdk.authorize() — that's the whole point
- Forget login_hint in BOTH the PAR body AND the auth redirect URL (prevents flash of email form)
close_reason: 306dbc3 Add ePDS PAR + redirect login route

title: Create app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts — token exchange + Redis session + user-did cookie
description:
## Files
- app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a GET route handler: `GET /api/oauth/callback?code=...&state=...`

This is the critical task — it does the manual token exchange and constructs a NodeSavedSession that the SDK can restore.

### Flow
1. Read `code` and `state` from query params. If either missing, return 400.
2. Read the `epds-oauth-session` cookie. If missing, return 400 ('No OAuth session found').
3. Call `decodeEpdsSessionCookie(cookieValue)` to get `{ state: savedState, codeVerifier, dpopPrivateJwk }`.
4. Verify `state === savedState`. If mismatch, return 400 ('State mismatch').
5. Call `restoreDpopKeyPair(dpopPrivateJwk)` to get `{ privateKey, publicJwk }`.
6. Build token body as URLSearchParams: grant_type='authorization_code', code, redirect_uri=getEpdsRedirectUri(), client_id=getEpdsClientId(), code_verifier=codeVerifier.
7. Call `fetchWithDpopRetry(tokenEndpoint, tokenBody, privateKey, publicJwk)`.
8. If token response is not ok, return 502 with error details.
9. Parse token response JSON. Expected shape: `{ access_token, token_type, scope, expires_in, sub, refresh_token? }`.
10. **Construct NodeSavedSession** and write to Redis:
```typescript
import { sessionStore } from '@/lib/hypercerts-sdk'

const nodeSavedSession = {
  dpopJwk: dpopPrivateJwk,  // the original private JWK from the cookie
  tokenSet: {
    iss: getEpdsEndpoints().tokenEndpoint.replace('/oauth/token', ''),  // issuer is the PDS origin
    sub: tokenResponse.sub,  // DID e.g. 'did:plc:abc123'
    aud: getEpdsEndpoints().tokenEndpoint.replace('/oauth/token', ''),  // audience = PDS
    scope: tokenResponse.scope || 'atproto transition:generic',
    access_token: tokenResponse.access_token,
    token_type: 'DPoP' as const,
    refresh_token: tokenResponse.refresh_token,
    expires_at: tokenResponse.expires_in
      ? new Date(Date.now() + tokenResponse.expires_in * 1000).toISOString()
      : undefined,
  },
}
await sessionStore.set(tokenResponse.sub, nodeSavedSession)
```
11. Create redirect response to `/`.
12. Set `user-did` cookie on the response (same as existing auth callback): httpOnly=true, secure=config.isProduction, maxAge=60*60*24*7, path='/'.
13. Delete the `epds-oauth-session` cookie (set maxAge=0).
14. Return the redirect.

### Critical notes on NodeSavedSession shape
- `dpopJwk` must be the **private** JWK (includes 'd' parameter) — this is what the SDK uses to create DPoP proofs for subsequent API calls
- `iss` (issuer) should be the PDS origin URL (e.g. 'https://pds-eu-west4.test.certified.app') — the SDK uses this to find the server metadata for token refresh
- `aud` should match the PDS URL that the DID resolves to — for ePDS users this is the same as iss
- `token_type` must be literally 'DPoP' (capital D, capital P)
- The `authMethod` field can be omitted (it's optional, defaults to legacy handling)

### Imports
- NextRequest, NextResponse from 'next/server'
- restoreDpopKeyPair, fetchWithDpopRetry from '@/lib/epds-helpers'
- getEpdsEndpoints, getEpdsClientId, getEpdsRedirectUri from '@/lib/epds-config'
- decodeEpdsSessionCookie, EPDS_SESSION_COOKIE_NAME from '@/lib/epds-session-cookie'
- sessionStore from '@/lib/hypercerts-sdk'
- config from '@/lib/config'

## Don't
- Use sdk.callback() — it won't work (state not in Redis state store)
- Generate a new DPoP key pair — MUST restore from the cookie (same key used in PAR)
- Forget to delete the epds-oauth-session cookie after use
- Forget the 'DPoP' token_type in the session (SDK will reject it otherwise)
close_reason: 5ced804 add ePDS OAuth callback route — token exchange + Redis session + user-did cookie

title: Fix async waterfalls in API routes: start auth promises early, await after validation
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/certs/create/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Apply the `async-api-routes` pattern: start expensive auth promises early, await them after synchronous validation.

### add-attachment/route.ts
Current: `getRepoContext()` is called on ~line 63 AFTER all form parsing and validation (lines 6-61).
Fix: Start `const ctxPromise = getRepoContext();` immediately after `const data = await req.formData();` (no await). Then `const ctx = await ctxPromise;` on the line where `getRepoContext()` is currently called. This overlaps auth network latency with CPU-bound parsing.

### add-location/route.ts
Current: `getAuthenticatedRepo()` is called on ~line 79 AFTER all form parsing and validation (lines 7-77).
Fix: Start `const repoPromise = getAuthenticatedRepo();` immediately after `const data = await req.formData();`. Then `const personalRepository = await repoPromise;` where the current await is.

### create/route.ts
Current: `getRepoContext()` is called on ~line 44 AFTER field parsing and validation (lines 8-42).
Fix: Start `const ctxPromise = getRepoContext();` immediately after `const formData = await req.formData();`. Then `const ctx = await ctxPromise;` where the current await is.

## Don't
- Change any error handling behavior
- Change any validation logic or order
- Move the `req.formData()` call — it must remain first since everything depends on it
- Add any new imports

title: Fix async waterfalls in profile update API routes: parallelize auth and formData
description:
## Files
- app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/profile/update/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Apply the `async-parallel` pattern: `getAuthenticatedRepo()` and `req.formData()` are independent async operations currently awaited sequentially.

### bsky/update/route.ts
Current (lines 8, 13):
```ts
const repo = await getAuthenticatedRepo();  // line 8
if (!repo) { return 401; }
const formData = await req.formData();      // line 13
```
Fix: Use start-early-await-late pattern to avoid parsing body for unauthenticated requests:
```ts
const repoPromise = getAuthenticatedRepo();
const formData = await req.formData();
const repo = await repoPromise;
if (!repo) {
  return NextResponse.json({ error: "Not authenticated" }, { status: 401 });
}
```

### update/route.ts
Current (lines 8, 13): Same sequential pattern.
Fix: Same start-early-await-late pattern as above.

## Don't
- Use Promise.all (we want to keep the early-exit guard for unauthenticated requests — avoid parsing body unnecessarily if auth fails fast)
- Change any error messages or status codes
- Change any validation logic after formData parsing
- Modify the rest of the function after the auth check
close_reason: d26d1ef parallelize auth and formData in profile update routes

title: Fix rendering-conditional-render: replace && with ternary in view components
description:
## Files
- components/hypercert-detail-view.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercert-evaluation-view.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercert-evidence-view.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercert-measurement-view.tsx (modify)
- components/hypercert-completion-step.tsx (modify)

## What to do
Replace `condition && (<JSX>)` patterns with `condition ? (<JSX>) : null` in all conditional renders where the condition could be a string or number (not already a boolean).

The `&&` operator can accidentally render `0` or `""` to the DOM when the left operand is falsy but not `false`/`null`/`undefined`.

### Rules:
1. If the condition is ALREADY a boolean expression (e.g., `isLoading && ...`, `step === 2 && ...`, `arr.length > 0 && ...`), convert to ternary for consistency but these are lower priority.
2. If the condition is a STRING variable (e.g., `description && ...`, `location && ...`, `imageUri && ...`), these MUST be converted because `""` would render to DOM.
3. If the condition is an OBJECT/ARRAY check (e.g., `evaluation.score != null && ...`), convert for consistency.

### Specific locations:

**hypercert-detail-view.tsx**: lines ~29, ~47, ~139
- `{imageUri && (` → `{imageUri ? (` ... `) : null}`
- `{record.shortDescription && (` → ternary
- `{record.description && (` → ternary

**hypercert-evaluation-view.tsx**: lines ~57, ~101, ~126, ~151
- `{evaluation.score != null && (` → ternary
- `{evaluation.content && evaluation.content.length > 0 && (` → ternary

- `{evaluation.location && (` → ternary

**hypercert-evidence-view.tsx**: lines ~76, ~83

- `{evidence.content && evidence.content.length > 0 && (` → ternary

**hypercert-measurement-view.tsx**: lines ~59, ~104

**hypercert-completion-step.tsx**: lines ~62, ~76, ~91, ~103, ~112
- `{hypercertInfo?.hypercertCid && (` → ternary
- `{!hypercertInfo?.hypercertCid && (` → ternary
- `{onBack && (` → ternary
- `{onCreateAnother && (` → ternary

## Don't
- Change any component logic or behavior
- Change any CSS classes or styling
- Add or remove any JSX elements
- Change any prop types or interfaces

title: Fix: dead `color: #ffffff` rule on ::after pseudo-element in atproto-branding.ts (from hypercerts-nyk.15)
description:
Review of hypercerts-nyk.15 found a dead CSS rule at line 167 of lib/atproto-branding.ts.

The rule:
```css
.grid h1.text-primary::after {
  ...
  color: #ffffff !important;  /* line 167 — DEAD RULE */
}
```

The `::after` pseudo-element has `content: ""` (empty string) and renders only a background-image (the certified-signin SVG). The `color` property has no effect on a pseudo-element with no text content. This is a leftover artifact from a previous iteration where the pseudo-element may have had text content.

Evidence: lib/atproto-branding.ts lines 156-168. The pseudo-element uses background-image, not text, so color is irrelevant.

Fix: Remove the `color: #ffffff !important` line from `.grid h1.text-primary::after`.

title: Epic: Handle OAuth rejection gracefully
description: When a user rejects the ATProto OAuth authorization, the callback URL includes error=access_denied. Currently the callback route lets sdk.callback() throw and returns raw JSON ({error, details}) to the browser — terrible UX. Goal: detect the error param early in the callback route, redirect the user back to / with a query param like ?auth_error=access_denied, and show a toast on the client side explaining what happened. The user should land back on the login screen seamlessly. Success: rejecting auth on the PDS shows a toast and the login dialog, not raw JSON. Scope: callback route early-return + client-side toast. Out of scope: new error pages, middleware, changes to the login flow itself.

title: Epic: Display contributors on hypercert detail page
description:
## Why
The hypercert detail page currently shows measurements, evidence, and evaluations but completely omits contributor information. The upstream hypercerts-lexicon (https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-lexicon) has evolved the activity schema: contributors are now inline on the activity record as an array of contributor objects (each with contributorIdentity, contributionWeight, contributionDetails). The local scaffold's generated lexicon types still use the old schema (contributions as StrongRef[] to separate records). We need to:

1. Update the local lexicon types to match the upstream activity.json schema
2. Build the full contributor display pipeline on the detail page
3. Support both inline contributor identities (DID strings) and StrongRef-based contributorInformation records
4. Resolve contributor DIDs to profile info (display name, avatar) via Bluesky public API

## What success looks like
- The hypercert detail page shows a 'Contributors' section between the metadata grid and the description
- Each contributor displays: identity (resolved to profile name/avatar when possible), role, and weight
- Contributors with DID identities link to their Bluesky profile
- The section gracefully handles: no contributors, contributors with only DID strings, contributors with StrongRef to contributorInformation records
- Loading and error states match the existing section patterns (measurements, evidence, evaluations)

## Key constraints
- Follow the exact same architectural patterns as measurements/evidence/evaluations sections
- Use the upstream lexicon schema from https://github.com/hypercerts-org/hypercerts-lexicon/blob/develop/lexicons/org/hypercerts/claim/activity.json as the source of truth
- Contributors are inline on the activity record (NOT fetched via Constellation backlinks like measurements/evidence)
- Must handle both contributorIdentity string format and StrongRef format
- Build and lint must pass after all tasks

title: Update activity lexicon types to match upstream contributor schema
notes: Cancelled: lexicon types come from @hypercerts-org/lexicon package, not local files. Replaced by mrb.7 (upgrade package).

title: Fix: contributor card link missing aria-label for accessibility (from hypercerts-mrb.4)
description: Review of hypercerts-mrb.4 found: In hypercert-contributor-view.tsx lines 86-93, the anchor tag wrapping the card for DID contributors has no aria-label. Screen readers will announce the full concatenated card text as the link label (name + handle + role + weight), which is verbose and confusing. The link should have an aria-label like 'View {name or identity} on Bluesky' to give screen reader users a concise, meaningful label. Additionally, the hover state only changes border color (transition-colors on the Card) but there is no visible focus ring for keyboard navigation — the card needs a focus-visible outline. Evidence: components/hypercert-contributor-view.tsx lines 84-97.

title: Fix: long displayName overflows contributor card on mobile (from hypercerts-mrb.4)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.4 found: In hypercert-contributor-view.tsx line 41, the name

element has no overflow/truncation classes. When profile.displayName is present (the non-truncated branch, lines 42-44), a long display name (e.g. 80+ characters) will overflow the card and break the flex layout, especially on mobile where the card is narrow. The spec (mrb.4 description) says to use truncate class with max-w-[200px] for the identity fallback, but the displayName branch has no such constraint. Fix: add truncate and a max-width to the outer

or the displayName span. Evidence: components/hypercert-contributor-view.tsx lines 41-49.

title: Fix: contributor count subtitle shows '0 contributors' in empty state (from hypercerts-mrb.5)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.5 found: In hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 45-48, the count subtitle is always rendered, even when displayContributors.length === 0. This means the section header reads 'Contributors / 0 contributors' before showing the empty state panel below. The measurements section (hypercert-measurements-section.tsx lines 71-75) and evidence section (hypercert-evidence-section.tsx lines 66-70) both conditionally render the count subtitle only when length > 0. Fix: wrap the count

in a conditional: {displayContributors.length > 0 && (...)}. Evidence: components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 45-48 vs hypercert-measurements-section.tsx lines 71-75.

title: Fix: contributor list uses array index as React key (from hypercerts-mrb.5)
description: Review of hypercerts-mrb.5 found: In hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 56 and 66, both the skeleton list and the contributor view list use key={index}. Using array index as key causes React reconciliation issues if the list is ever filtered, sorted, or reordered — components will not remount correctly and may show stale data. The contributor identity string (contributor.identity) is a more stable and semantically meaningful key. Fix: change key={index} to key={contributor.identity} on line 66 (and key={} is acceptable for skeletons since they are transient). Evidence: components/hypercert-contributors-section.tsx lines 55-57 and 66-72.

Fix: Collections enum uses wrong NSID for 'contribution' and 'evidence' (from hypercerts-mrb.7)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.7 found: `lib/types.ts` lines 48-53 defines the `Collections` enum. Two entries are mismatched against the installed `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` package:

1. `contribution = "org.hypercerts.claim.contribution"` — The package has BOTH `org.hypercerts.claim.contribution` (old schema: `contributors: string[]`, `description`) AND `org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails` (new schema: `role`, `contributionDescription`, `startDate`, `endDate`). `lib/queries.ts` line 4 imports `OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails as HypercertContribution` and uses it for `createContribution` (line 84-94), but `Collections.contribution` still points to the OLD collection NSID `org.hypercerts.claim.contribution`. Records created via `createContribution` will be written to the wrong collection.

2. `evidence = "org.hypercerts.claim.evidence"` — The package's `OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment` (imported as `HypercertEvidence` in `lib/queries.ts` line 5) has `$type: 'org.hypercerts.claim.attachment'`, but `Collections.evidence` is `"org.hypercerts.claim.evidence"`. Records created via `createEvidence` (line 108-118) will be written to `org.hypercerts.claim.evidence` but the type system expects `org.hypercerts.claim.attachment`.

Evidence:
- `lib/types.ts` line 49: `contribution = "org.hypercerts.claim.contribution"`
- `lib/types.ts` line 50: `evidence = "org.hypercerts.claim.evidence"`
- `lib/queries.ts` line 4: `OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails as HypercertContribution`
- `lib/queries.ts` line 5: `OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment as HypercertEvidence`
- Package NSID constants: `CONTRIBUTION_DETAILS_NSID = "org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails"`, `ATTACHMENT_NSID = "org.hypercerts.claim.attachment"`

Fix: Update `Collections` enum:
```ts
contribution = "org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails",
evidence = "org.hypercerts.claim.attachment",
```
Verify all callers of `Collections.contribution` and `Collections.evidence` still work after the rename. This is a runtime data-corruption bug — records are being written to the wrong AT Protocol collection.

title: Fix: blob-display.tsx $type check uses wrong value for Uri type (from hypercerts-mrb.7)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.7 found: `components/blob-display.tsx` line 19 checks:
```ts
if (type === "app.certified.defs#uri") {
```

But the installed `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` package defines `OrgHypercertsDefs.Uri` with:
```ts
$type?: 'org.hypercerts.defs#uri';
```
(see `node_modules/@hypercerts-org/lexicon/dist/types/org/hypercerts/defs.d.ts` line 7)

The check uses `app.certified.defs#uri` but the actual $type value in records is `org.hypercerts.defs#uri`. This means the URI branch will never match, and all URI-type content will fall through to the final `return "—"`, silently hiding evidence/attachment links.

Similarly, the blob type check on line 24:
```ts
if (["smallBlob", "largeBlob", "blob"].includes(type || "")) {
```
This checks for bare names like `"smallBlob"` but the actual $type values are `"org.hypercerts.defs#smallBlob"` and `"org.hypercerts.defs#largeBlob"`. This branch also never matches.

Evidence:
- `components/blob-display.tsx` lines 19, 24
- `node_modules/@hypercerts-org/lexicon/dist/types/org/hypercerts/defs.d.ts` lines 7, 15, 23

Fix: Update the type checks to use the correct fully-qualified $type strings:
```ts
if (type === "org.hypercerts.defs#uri") { ... }
if (["org.hypercerts.defs#smallBlob", "org.hypercerts.defs#largeBlob"].includes(type || "")) { ... }
```

title: Fix: query key namespace collision — contributorProfile shares 'hypercerts' root with record-scoped keys (from hypercerts-mrb.3)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.3 found: `lib/api/query-keys.ts` line 38-39 adds:
```ts
contributorProfile: (did: string) =>
  [...queryKeys.hypercerts.all, "contributor-profile", did] as const,
```

This produces keys like `["hypercerts", "contributor-profile", "did:plc:abc"]`. However, there is already a separate `external.bluesky.profile` key (line 46-48) for the same Bluesky profile data:
```ts
profile: (actor: string) =>
  [...queryKeys.external.bluesky.all, "profile", actor] as const,
```

Two different query keys now cache the same underlying data (Bluesky profile for a DID). If any other part of the app uses `queryKeys.external.bluesky.profile(did)` to fetch or invalidate a profile, the contributor profile cache will not be invalidated, and vice versa. This is a cache coherence issue.

Additionally, the `contributorProfile` key is semantically an external Bluesky API call, not a hypercert record operation. Placing it under `queryKeys.hypercerts` is a namespace violation that will confuse future developers.

Fix: Either (a) reuse `queryKeys.external.bluesky.profile(did)` in `useContributorProfilesQuery` instead of introducing a new key, or (b) move `contributorProfile` under `queryKeys.external.bluesky` and document the deduplication intent.

title: Fix: getProfile() throws on non-200 but retry:false leaves error state permanent (from hypercerts-mrb.3)
description:
Review of hypercerts-mrb.3 found: `queries/hypercerts/use-contributor-profiles-query.ts` line 24 sets `retry: false`. The `getProfile` function in `lib/api/external/bluesky.ts` line 13 calls `externalApiClient` which throws an `APIError` on any non-2xx response (see `lib/api/client.ts` lines 132-136). With `retry: false`, a transient network error or a 429 rate-limit from the Bluesky public API will permanently mark the query as `isError` for the lifetime of the component. The contributor profile will never be retried even after the rate limit window expires.

The spec comment says 'Failed lookups are silently ignored' — this is partially correct (the Map simply won't contain the DID), but the query stays in error state and will not re-attempt on the next mount.

Evidence:
- `use-contributor-profiles-query.ts` line 24: `retry: false`
- `lib/api/client.ts` lines 132-136: throws `APIError` on non-ok response
- No `throwOnError: false` or error boundary present

Fix: Consider `retry: 1` with exponential backoff for transient errors, or at minimum add `staleTime: 0` so a remount will re-attempt. Alternatively, wrap `queryFn` to catch and return `null` instead of throwing, making the error truly silent and allowing future retries.

title: Upgrade @hypercerts-org/lexicon to beta.15 and migrate local lexicon imports
description:
## Files
- package.json (modify)
- lib/utils.ts (modify)
- lib/queries.ts (modify)
- lib/types.ts (modify)
- components/hypercerts-base-form.tsx (modify)
- components/blob-display.tsx (modify)

## What to do
The local `lexicons/` directory contains generated types from an outdated version of `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` (0.10.0-beta.3). The installed `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` package needs upgrading to `0.10.0-beta.15` which has the `contributors` field on the activity record. After upgrading, migrate all imports from local `@/lexicons/types/...` to the package.

### Step 1: Upgrade the package
```bash
npm install @hypercerts-org/lexicon@0.10.0-beta.15
```

### Step 2: Migrate imports in 5 files

The SDK re-exports all lexicon types. Use `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core` or `@hypercerts-org/lexicon` for imports. The SDK already re-exports everything via:
```typescript
export { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity, OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment, OrgHypercertsClaimCollection, OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails, OrgHypercertsClaimContributorInformation, OrgHypercertsClaimEvaluation, OrgHypercertsClaimMeasurement, OrgHypercertsClaimRights, OrgHypercertsDefs, AppCertifiedLocation, ComAtprotoRepoStrongRef } from "@hypercerts-org/lexicon";
```

**Migration mapping:**
| Old import | New import |
|---|---|
| `import * as Hypercert from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/activity"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity as Hypercert } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertClaim from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/activity"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimActivity as HypercertClaim } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as Contribution from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/contribution"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails as Contribution } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertContribution from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/contribution"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails as HypercertContribution } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as Evaluation from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/evaluation"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimEvaluation as Evaluation } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertEvaluation from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/evaluation"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimEvaluation as HypercertEvaluation } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertEvidence from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/evidence"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimAttachment as HypercertEvidence } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertRights from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/claim/rights"` | `import { OrgHypercertsClaimRights as HypercertRights } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import * as HypercertLocation from "@/lexicons/types/app/certified/location"` | `import { AppCertifiedLocation as HypercertLocation } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` |
| `import { SmallBlob, Uri } from "@/lexicons/types/org/hypercerts/defs"` | `import { OrgHypercertsDefs } from "@hypercerts-org/sdk-core"` then use `OrgHypercertsDefs.SmallBlob` and `OrgHypercertsDefs.Uri` |

**IMPORTANT**: The old local `contribution.ts` had a different schema (single `org.hypercerts.claim.contribution` record with `hypercert`, `role`, `contributors[]`, `description`). The new package has `OrgHypercertsClaimContributionDetails` (with `role`, `contributionDescription`, `startDate`, `endDate`). Check that the usages in `lib/types.ts` and `lib/queries.ts` still compile — the `HypercertContributionData` type alias may need its `value` type updated.

### Step 3: Fix any type mismatches
After migration, run `npx tsc --noEmit` and fix any type errors caused by schema differences between the old local types and the new package types. Common issues:
- `workScope` changed from `string` to a union type
- `workTimeFrameFrom`/`workTimeFrameTo` renamed to `startDate`/`endDate`
- `contributions` (old StrongRef[]) vs `contributors` (new inline Contributor[])
- `location` (singular) vs `locations` (plural array)
- `evidence` field removed from activity (evidence is now via attachments)

For each breaking change, update the consuming code to handle both old and new field names where possible, or update to the new names if the old ones are unused.

### Step 4: Verify
```bash
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run build
```

## Don't
- Delete the local `lexicons/` directory yet (other tasks may still reference it during transition; a separate cleanup task can handle removal)
- Upgrade `@hypercerts-org/sdk-core` (only upgrade the lexicon package)
- Change any component rendering logic (only imports and type references)
- Break existing functionality — if a type mismatch is too complex, add a `// TODO: update when SDK catches up` comment and use type assertion

title: Retheme PDS branding CSS to flow with PDS design system instead of fighting it
description:
## Files
- lib/atproto-branding.ts (modify)

## Context
The current branding CSS forces `color-scheme: light only` and overrides nearly every color class with hardcoded values and `!important`. This fights the PDS's own design system and causes issues like dark-on-dark text when we set the left panel to a dark color.

The PDS layout uses hardcoded Tailwind `slate-*` classes (NOT the `--color-contrast-*` scale) for the sign-in page. The left panel is `md:bg-slate-100` (`#f1f5f9`), the right panel inherits `bg-white`. The `--branding-color-primary` variable controls `text-primary` (H1 title), `bg-primary` (buttons), and the contrast scale hue for nav/footer/cards.

## What to do
Refactor `generateBrandingCss()` to work WITH the PDS instead of against it. The goal: set the right CSS variables so the PDS derives a navy-blue-tinted theme naturally, then only add targeted overrides for things the PDS can't do on its own.

### 1. Set branding CSS variables to navy theme
Replace the current `:root` block (lines ~68-83) with:
```css
:root {
  --branding-color-primary: 15 37 68;
  --branding-color-primary-contrast: 255 255 255;
  --branding-color-primary-hue: 216;
  --branding-color-error: 229 72 77;
  --branding-color-error-contrast: 255 255 255;
  --branding-color-error-hue: 358;
  --branding-color-warning: 245 159 0;
  --branding-color-warning-contrast: 43 45 49;
  --branding-color-warning-hue: 38;
  --branding-color-success: 43 138 62;
  --branding-color-success-contrast: 255 255 255;
  --branding-color-success-hue: 134;
}
```
This makes:
- `text-primary` / `bg-primary` = dark navy (`#0F2544`)
- `text-primary-contrast` = white
- The contrast scale gets a subtle navy-blue tint (hue 216)
- Buttons become navy instead of charcoal

### 2. Remove `color-scheme: light only !important`
Delete the `color-scheme` forcing. Let the PDS respect the user's system preference or its own defaults.

### 3. Left panel dark override (KEEP)
Keep the `md:bg-slate-100` override to `#0F2544` — this is necessary because the PDS hardcodes `slate-100` for the left panel.

### 4. Left panel text overrides (KEEP)
Keep the scoped white text rules for `.md\\:bg-slate-100` — these are necessary for the dark panel.

### 5. REMOVE most global color overrides
Delete the following sections that fight the PDS's native styling:
- `/* FORCE LIGHT COLOR SCHEME */` — remove `color-scheme` forcing
- `/* MAIN BACKGROUNDS */` — remove body, `.bg-white`, `.dark .bg-white` overrides. Let the PDS handle these.
- `/* TEXT COLORS - LIGHT THEME */` — remove `.text-slate-900`, `.text-slate-600`, etc. overrides. The PDS's own Tailwind slate colors are fine.
- `/* ACCENT/PRIMARY COLORS */` — remove `.text-primary`, `.bg-primary`, `.border-primary` overrides. These now come from the CSS variables.
- `/* FORM INPUTS */` — remove input/textarea/select overrides. Let PDS style them.
- `/* USERNAME PREVIEW & PASSWORD STRENGTH */` — remove `.bg-gray-200`, `.bg-gray-300`, text-gray-* overrides.
- `/* ACCOUNT SELECTION & FORM ITEMS */` — remove `.bg-gray-100` overrides.
- `/* CHECKBOXES */` — remove. PDS handles these via `accent-color` from primary.
- `/* INFO/WARNING/ERROR BOXES */` — remove `.bg-contrast-*` overrides. The contrast scale now has the right hue.
- `/* BUTTONS */` — remove the generic button color overrides (`.bg-primary`, hover states). Keep ONLY the button text/icon replacement rules (the `::before` and `::after` for 'Sign in with Certified').
- `/* LINKS */` — remove. Let PDS handle link colors.
- `/* FORM TEXT ELEMENTS */` — remove.
- `/* DROPDOWN/SELECT */` — remove.
- `/* ERROR PAGE */` — remove `.text-4xl`, `.text-5xl` overrides.
- `/* Input container */` overrides — remove all the `.rounded-lg` input container styling.
- `/* Text that should be dark on light backgrounds */` — remove the `.text-white` override (line ~444) which forces `.text-white` to dark. This was needed when forcing light mode but is harmful now.

### 6. KEEP these sections
- Logo replacement (`img[alt="Hypercerts Logo"]`) — PDS can't do this natively
- H1 text replacement with `certified-signin.svg` — PDS can't do this
- Button text/icon replacement (`::before`/`::after`) — PDS can't do this
- Left panel dark background + scoped light text
- Mobile dark heading area
- Authenticate page logo and button replacements
- Error page logo suppression
- Desktop/mobile alignment rules

### 7. Secondary button overrides for the submit button
The submit button `button[type="submit"]` styling can be simplified. Since `bg-primary` now resolves to navy via the CSS variable, the button will naturally be navy. Keep only the text/icon replacement (`font-size: 0`, `::before`, `::after`).

### 8. Right panel lighter shade
The right panel (`bg-white`) will stay white by default. If you want it to be a lighter shade of navy instead of pure white, add:
```css
.bg-white,
.dark .bg-white {
  background-color: hsl(216 20% 97%) !important;
}
```
This gives a very subtle navy-tinted off-white that flows with the left panel. But this is optional — pure white also works.

## Don't
- Remove the logo/image replacement CSS
- Remove the button text replacement CSS
- Remove the left panel dark background override
- Change the SVG asset URLs
- Touch any files other than lib/atproto-branding.ts
close_reason: ce6a9cf refactor: retheme PDS branding CSS to flow with PDS design system

title: Clean up PDS branding CSS: fix broad selector, remove dead code, consolidate dark mode blocks
description:
## Files
- lib/atproto-branding.ts (modify)

## What to do
Address 6 CSS quality issues found during review. Bundle them into a single cleanup pass.

### 1. Fix overly broad `img[alt*="Logo"]` selector (P3 — hypercerts-5jk)
The substring match `alt*="Logo"` could accidentally affect any image with 'Logo' in its alt text on PDS pages. Change to exact match only:
```css
/* BEFORE */
img[alt="Hypercerts Logo"],
img[alt*="Logo"] {

/* AFTER */
img[alt="Hypercerts Logo"] {
```
Remove the `img[alt*="Logo"]` selector everywhere it appears (base rule, mobile rule, and the dark mode `filter: invert` rule). The exact `img[alt="Hypercerts Logo"]` match is sufficient.

### 2. Remove dead `color: #ffffff` on `::after` (P3 — hypercerts-jgh)
The `.grid h1.text-primary::after` has `color: #ffffff !important` but `content: ""` — no text to color. Remove the dead property:
```css
.grid h1.text-primary::after {
  content: "" !important;
  /* ... */
  /* color: #ffffff !important;  <-- DELETE */
}
```

### 3. Consolidate dark mode blocks (P4 — hypercerts-c3t)
There are 5 scattered `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` blocks throughout the file. Merge them into a single block at the end of the CSS (before the closing backtick). This makes dark mode behavior easy to find and maintain.

The consolidated block should contain ALL dark-mode-only rules:
- Left panel background override (`#0F2544`)
- Left panel white text overrides
- Left panel subtitle text overrides
- Logo `filter: invert(1)`
- Authenticate page logo `filter: invert(1)`
- Authenticate page H1 `color: #ffffff`
- H1 `certified-signin.svg` (dark variant) override
- Button bright blue override (`#3B82F6`)
- Mobile dark heading (combined with `max-width: 767px`)

### 4. Remove unnecessary `position: relative` (P4 — hypercerts-84k)
The `main.flex.flex-col.items-center > h1.text-primary { position: relative !important; }` rule is unnecessary — the `::before` pseudo-element is block-flow, not absolutely positioned. Remove it.

### 5. Remove redundant `width: 32px` override (P4 — hypercerts-ua9)
The `div.w-8` container is already 32px in Tailwind. The override `width: 32px !important` is a no-op. Also remove the dead `object-position: left center` on `img[alt="Ma Earth"]` (32x32 square, object-position has no effect with `object-fit: contain`).

### 6. Remove redundant mobile `background-position` (P4 — hypercerts-c7v)
The mobile override repeats `background-position: left center` which is already set in the base rule. Remove the redundant line from the mobile media query.

## Don't
- Change any functional behavior (colors, sizes, images, selectors that work correctly)
- Change the `:root` CSS variables
- Change SVG asset URLs
- Reorder rules in a way that changes specificity/cascade behavior
- Touch any files other than lib/atproto-branding.ts

title: Fix: Remove Ma Earth CSS selectors from atproto-branding.ts (copy-paste artifact)
description:
Review of the scaffold codebase found Ma Earth references in lib/atproto-branding.ts — a copy-paste artifact from the maearth-app project.

## Findings

All 4 references are in `lib/atproto-branding.ts`:

- **Line 112** (comment): `/* The small logo on the Authorize page has alt="Ma Earth" and is in a constrained container */`
- **Line 113** (functional CSS selector): `img[alt="Ma Earth"] {`
- **Line 121** (functional CSS selector): `.flex.items-center.justify-start.gap-2 > div.w-8:has(img[alt="Ma Earth"]),`
- **Line 122** (functional CSS selector): `div.w-8:has(img[alt="Ma Earth"]) {`

Lines 113, 121, and 122 are **functional code** — CSS selectors that target an image with `alt="Ma Earth"`. In this scaffold, the authorize-page logo will have a different alt text, so these selectors are dead code that will never match. The comment on line 112 also incorrectly references Ma Earth.

## Fix

Replace the `alt="Ma Earth"` selectors with a generic selector that matches any small logo image on the Authorize page, or use the actual alt text that the scaffold's PDS OAuth page renders (e.g., `img[alt*="Logo"]` or `img[alt="Hypercerts"]`). Update the comment accordingly.

The block spans lines 111–125 of `lib/atproto-branding.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `rg -i 'ma.?earth|maearth|ma_earth' lib/atproto-branding.ts` returns no matches
2. The CSS block that previously targeted `img[alt="Ma Earth"]` is replaced with a generic or scaffold-appropriate selector
3. The comment on line 112 no longer mentions Ma Earth
4. The file still compiles without TypeScript errors (`pnpm tsc --noEmit`)
5. The branding CSS still covers the Authorize page small logo use-case (selector targets something meaningful)

title: Add buildClientMetadata function to lib/config.ts
description:
## Files
- lib/config.ts (modify)

## What to do
Add a `buildClientMetadata()` function to `lib/config.ts` that returns the full OAuth client metadata object. This function replaces the inline object currently in `app/client-metadata.json/route.ts`.

**Why:** The metadata endpoint currently builds its response inline. We need a reusable function that conditionally includes branding CSS in production mode.

**Function signature:** `export function buildClientMetadata(): Record<string, unknown>`

**Behavior:**
1. If `config.isLoopback` is true (local dev), return metadata WITHOUT branding:
   ```
   {
     client_id: config.clientId,
     client_name: "Hypercerts Scaffold",
     client_uri: config.baseUrl,
     redirect_uris: [config.redirectUri],
     scope: OAUTH_SCOPE,
     logo_uri: `${config.baseUrl}/favicon.ico`,
     grant_types: ["authorization_code", "refresh_token"],
     response_types: ["code"],
     token_endpoint_auth_method: "none",
     application_type: "native",
     dpop_bound_access_tokens: true,
   }
   ```

2. If NOT loopback (production/preview), return metadata WITH branding:
   ```
   {
     client_id: config.clientId,
     client_name: "Hypercerts Scaffold",
     client_uri: config.baseUrl,
     redirect_uris: [config.redirectUri],
     scope: OAUTH_SCOPE,
     logo_uri: `${config.baseUrl}/favicon.ico`,
     grant_types: ["authorization_code", "refresh_token"],
     response_types: ["code"],
     token_endpoint_auth_method: "none",
     application_type: "web",
     dpop_bound_access_tokens: true,
     branding: {
       css: generateBrandingCss(config.baseUrl),
     },
   }
   ```

**Important:** Keep `token_endpoint_auth_method: "none"` in BOTH modes. Upgrading to `private_key_jwt` is a separate concern and out of scope for this task.

**Import:** Add `import { generateBrandingCss } from "./atproto-branding";` at the top of the file.

**Placement:** Add the function after the `config` export (around line 204), before the env var validation block.

## Dont
- Do not change token_endpoint_auth_method (keep "none" in both modes)
- Do not modify the config object itself
- Do not modify the metadata route file (separate task)
- Do not change any existing functions (isLoopback, getBaseUrl, etc.)
- Do not remove any existing exports

title: Update client-metadata.json route to use buildClientMetadata
description:
## Files
- app/client-metadata.json/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
Replace the inline metadata object in the route handler with a call to `buildClientMetadata()`.

**Current code (replace entirely):**
```typescript
import { config, OAUTH_SCOPE } from "@/lib/config";
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

export async function GET() {
  return NextResponse.json({
    client_id: config.clientId,
    client_name: "Hypercert Scaffold",
    client_uri: config.baseUrl,
    redirect_uris: [config.redirectUri],
    scope: OAUTH_SCOPE,
    logo_uri:`${config.baseUrl}/favicon.ico`,
    grant_types: ["authorization_code", "refresh_token"],
    response_types: ["code"],
    token_endpoint_auth_method: "none",
    application_type: config.isLoopback ? "native" : "web",
    dpop_bound_access_tokens: true,
  });
}
```

**New code:**
```typescript
import { buildClientMetadata } from "@/lib/config";
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

/**
 * OAuth Client Metadata Endpoint
 *
 * Returns OAuth client metadata according to RFC 7591.
 * In production mode, includes custom branding CSS for PDS OAuth pages.
 * In loopback mode, returns minimal metadata (PDS ignores it anyway).
 *
 * @see https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7591
 */
export async function GET() {
  return NextResponse.json(buildClientMetadata());
}
```

## Dont
- Do not add any logic to this file — all logic lives in buildClientMetadata
- Do not import config or OAUTH_SCOPE directly (buildClientMetadata handles it)
- Do not add error handling (the metadata is static/deterministic)

title: Document ngrok setup for local branding testing
description:
## Files

## What to do
Add a section to DEVELOPMENT.md documenting how to test OAuth branding locally using ngrok.

**Context:** ATProto OAuth loopback mode (localhost) cannot support branding CSS — the PDS auto-generates minimal metadata and ignores custom fields. To test branding locally, developers need a public HTTPS URL via a tunnel service.

**Section to add (under a new heading "Testing OAuth Branding Locally"):**

   - Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=https://abc123.ngrok-free.app` in `.env.local`
   - Restart dev server
   - The app now enters production mode and serves full metadata with branding at `/client-metadata.json`
3. Note that `next.config.ts` may need `allowedDevOrigins` configured for ngrok domains (reference existing task hypercerts-scaffold-r25 if it exists)
4. Note that branding is automatically active on Vercel preview/production deployments — no ngrok needed there

**Placement:** Add after the existing OAuth/authentication section in DEVELOPMENT.md. If no such section exists, add near the end before any appendix/reference sections.

## Dont

description:

Random ngrok URLs or unregistered Vercel preview URLs will NOT show branding even though the metadata is served correctly.

title: Sanitize OAuth error_description in auth callback
description:
## Files
- app/api/auth/callback/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: security issue at line 18.

**Original comment:**
> The error_description on line 12 is passed through from the authorization server verbatim. While React will safely render it as text (no XSS), the content is controlled by the external OAuth provider and could be unexpectedly verbose or confusing to end users. Consider capping or sanitizing it, or using a fixed user-friendly message like the catch block does.

**Suggested fix:**
On line 12, after getting the error_description from searchParams, sanitize it before using it in the redirect:
1. Trim whitespace
2. Enforce a max length of 200 characters — if it exceeds, truncate and append '...'
3. If empty after trimming, use the fallback 'Authorization was cancelled' (already the current fallback)
4. Keep the full raw provider message in the console.warn on line 13 for server-side debugging
5. Use the sanitized version in the redirect URL searchParams on line 16

title: Fix unsafe error casts across 4 API routes
description:
## Files
- app/api/certs/add-attachment/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/certs/add-location/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/profile/bsky/update/route.ts (modify)
- app/api/profile/update/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged 4 related error-handling issues — all are unsafe `(e as Error).message` casts that produce `undefined` when a non-Error value is thrown.

1. [error-handling] Line 141 in add-attachment/route.ts: `(e as Error).message` in catch block
2. [error-handling] Line 97 in add-location/route.ts: `(e as Error).message` in catch block — also leaks internal details via `details` field
3. [error-handling] Line 102 in bsky/update/route.ts: `(error as Error).message` in catch block
4. [error-handling] Line 120 in profile/update/route.ts: `(error as Error).message` in catch block

**Suggested fixes:**

For all 4 files, replace the unsafe cast with a safe pattern:
```typescript
e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)
```

Specific changes:

1. add-attachment/route.ts line 141:
   ```diff
   -{ error: \`Failed to add attachment: ${(e as Error).message}\` }
   +{ error: \`Failed to add attachment: ${e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e)}\` }
   ```

   ```diff
   -{ error: 'Internal server error', details: (e as Error).message }
   ```

3. bsky/update/route.ts line 102:
   ```diff
   -{ error: \`Profile update failed: ${(error as Error).message}\` }
   +{ error: \`Profile update failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}\` }
   ```

4. profile/update/route.ts line 120:
   ```diff
   -{ error: \`Profile update failed: ${(error as Error).message}\` }
   +{ error: \`Profile update failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}\` }
   ```

title: Add JWK structure validation in jwks.json route
description:
## Files
- app/jwks.json/route.ts (modify)

## What to do
CodeRabbit flagged: error-handling issue at line 54.

**Original comment:**
> Unvalidated privateKey.keys will throw an unhelpful 500 if the JWK structure is unexpected. If ATPROTO_JWK_PRIVATE is valid JSON but doesn't contain a keys array (e.g., it's a single JWK object instead of a JWK Set), (privateKey.keys as Array<...>).map(...) will throw a TypeError that isn't caught.

**Suggested fix:**
After the JSON.parse on line 25 and before the PRIVATE_KEY_FIELDS line on line 37, add a guard:
```typescript
if (
  !privateKey.keys ||
  !Array.isArray(privateKey.keys) ||
  privateKey.keys.length === 0
) {
  return NextResponse.json(
    { error: "ATPROTO_JWK_PRIVATE must contain a JWKS with a non-empty 'keys' array." },
    { status: 500 },
  );
}
```

**Context:**
The route serves the public JWKS endpoint. Line 38 does `(privateKey.keys as Array<...>).map(...)` which will throw a raw TypeError if keys is undefined or not an array. The existing validation checks for missing env var (line 16) and invalid JSON (line 26) but not for invalid JWK structure.

title: Remove personal email addresses from .beads/issues.jsonl — replace with GitHub handles
description:
## Files
- .beads/issues.jsonl (modify)

## Context
The issues.jsonl file contains personal email addresses in the 'owner' fields that are committed to the public git repo, exposing PII:
- `kzoepa@gmail.com` appears as owner on ~26 issues (lines 1, 6-19, 20, 32-40, 46)
- `adam@hypercerts.org` appears as owner on 5 issues (lines 41-45)

The JSONL is auto-exported from the beads SQLite DB. However, the JSONL also serves as an import source (config says `no-auto-import: false`). So editing the JSONL and re-importing should work.

## What to do

### 1. Replace email addresses in issues.jsonl
Use sed or similar to do a global find-and-replace in `.beads/issues.jsonl`:
- Replace `"owner":"kzoepa@gmail.com"` with `"owner":"kzoeps"`
- Replace `"owner":"adam@hypercerts.org"` with `"owner":"aspiers"`

### 2. Also check and replace in created_by fields
Check if `created_by` fields also contain emails. From the data I see `"created_by":"kzoeps"` and `"created_by":"Adam Spiers"` — these are names/handles, not emails, so they should be fine. But verify.

### 3. Check interactions.jsonl too
Search `.beads/interactions.jsonl` for any email addresses and replace similarly.

### 4. Verify the JSONL is still valid
After editing, run `hb list` to verify the beads DB can still read the data. If `hb sync` overwrites your changes, you may need to:
- Delete the SQLite DB (`rm .beads/beads.db`)
- Run `hb init` to re-initialize from the JSONL
- Run `hb sync` to verify

### 5. Do a repo-wide search
Run `rg -i 'kzoepa@gmail|adam@hypercerts' --type-not binary` to check for any other occurrences outside .beads/.

### 6. Note about git history
The emails are already in git history. A `git filter-repo` rewrite would be needed to fully remove them from history, but that's destructive and out of scope for this task. Just fix the current files. Add a comment in the commit message noting that history rewriting may be needed if this is a concern.

## Don't
- Run git filter-repo or any history-rewriting commands
- Modify any code files
- Change the beads config
- Delete or recreate issues — only change the owner field values

title: Epic: Add Prettier, Husky, and lint-staged for pre-commit checks
description: The project has ESLint configured (flat config, eslint-config-next) but no Prettier installed (the existing .prettierrc.json has a typo and prettier isn't even a dependency), no husky, and no lint-staged. Goal: install prettier with a proper config, install husky + lint-staged, and wire them together so that on every commit: (1) staged .ts/.tsx/.mjs files are auto-formatted with prettier --write, (2) staged .ts/.tsx files are linted with eslint --fix. The existing .prettierrc.json needs to be fixed (single → singleQuote). Success: git commit triggers pre-commit hook that formats and lints staged files. Scope: prettier config, .prettierignore, husky setup, lint-staged config, package.json scripts. Out of scope: changing ESLint rules, adding new ESLint plugins, reformatting the entire codebase in this epic.

title: Install and configure Prettier, Husky, and lint-staged
description:
## Files
- package.json (modify — add devDependencies, scripts, lint-staged config)
- .prettierrc.json (modify — fix typo, add full config)
- .prettierignore (create)
- .husky/pre-commit (create via husky init)
- eslint.config.mjs (modify — add eslint-config-prettier to disable formatting rules that conflict with prettier)

## What to do

### 1. Install dependencies

```bash
npm install --save-dev prettier husky lint-staged eslint-config-prettier
```

### 2. Fix and expand .prettierrc.json

Replace the current broken config with:
```json
{
  "semi": true,
  "singleQuote": false,
  "tabWidth": 2,
  "trailingComma": "all",
  "printWidth": 80,
  "plugins": [],
  "endOfLine": "lf"
}
```

Note: `singleQuote: false` preserves the existing codebase convention (double quotes are used everywhere). The old config had `"single": false` which is not a valid Prettier key.

### 3. Create .prettierignore

```
node_modules/
.next/
out/
build/
coverage/
*.tgz
pnpm-lock.yaml
package-lock.json
.beads/
vendor/
```

### 4. Add eslint-config-prettier to ESLint config

In `eslint.config.mjs`, import and add `eslint-config-prettier` to disable ESLint rules that conflict with Prettier. The config should look like:

```js
import { defineConfig, globalIgnores } from "eslint/config";
import nextVitals from "eslint-config-next/core-web-vitals";
import nextTs from "eslint-config-next/typescript";
import prettierConfig from "eslint-config-prettier";

const eslintConfig = defineConfig([
  ...nextVitals,
  ...nextTs,
  prettierConfig,
  globalIgnores([
    ".next/**",
    "out/**",
    "build/**",
    "next-env.d.ts",
  ]),
]);

export default eslintConfig;
```

### 5. Initialize Husky

```bash
npx husky init
```

This creates `.husky/pre-commit`. Replace its contents with:

```bash
npx lint-staged
```

### 6. Add lint-staged config to package.json

Add this to package.json at the top level:

```json
"lint-staged": {
  "*.{ts,tsx,mjs}": [
    "prettier --write"
  ],
  "*.{ts,tsx}": [
    "eslint --fix"
  ]
}
```

### 7. Add scripts to package.json

Add these scripts (keep existing ones):

```json
"format": "prettier --write .",
"format:check": "prettier --check .",
"prepare": "husky"
```

The `prepare` script ensures husky hooks are installed after `npm install`.

### 8. Verify

- Run `npx prettier --check .` — it will likely show files that need formatting (that is OK, we are not reformatting in this task)
- Run `npm run lint` — should pass
- Run `npx tsc --noEmit` — should pass
- Test the hook: stage a file, commit, verify lint-staged runs

## Don't
- Do NOT reformat the entire codebase (that is a separate task)
- Do NOT change any ESLint rules (only add eslint-config-prettier to disable conflicting formatting rules)
- Do NOT add prettier plugins (like prettier-plugin-tailwindcss) — keep it simple
- Do NOT modify any source files (.ts, .tsx) in this task
close_reason: d1d8dc4 feat: install and configure Prettier, Husky, and lint-staged
